entry.title: Azure Digital Twins
entry.description: Azure Digital Twins enables modeling, simulating, and analyzing real-world environments for IoT and smart spaces applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10,000 API calls per month and 2,500 query units per month.
caveats: Overages are billed at standard rates.
Free tier applies per Azure subscription.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free limits incurs charges automatically.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Azure Digital Twins instance.
Define your digital twin models using the modeling language.
Ingest data from IoT devices or simulated sources.
Query and visualize your digital twin graph.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to model, simulate, or analyze physical environments for IoT or smart building projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require a fully free solution for high-volume workloads or lack Azure integration in your stack.
avoidIf: You do not use Azure services
entry.body: Azure Digital Twins provides a scalable platform for modeling and simulating real-world environments, ideal for IoT and smart space applications with a generous free tier for prototyping.

entry.title: Codeac.io
entry.description: Codeac.io provides automated code review and static analysis for infrastructure-as-code and software projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories and small private projects.
caveats: Some advanced features require paid plan

useCases: Automated code review for open source projects
Improving code quality in small teams
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Codeac.io account using GitHub or GitLab.
Authorize Codeac.io to access your repositories.
Select the repository you want to analyze.
Configure analysis settings as needed.
Review automated feedback and fix detected issues.
entry.whenToUse: Use Codeac.io to automate code quality checks for public repositories or small private projects, especially for infrastructure-as-code.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need support for multiple private repositories or require advanced features for large teams.
entry.body: Codeac.io offers automated code review and static analysis for infrastructure-as-code and software projects, with a free tier for public and small private repositories.

entry.title: CodeFactor
entry.description: CodeFactor provides automated code review and quality analysis for GitHub and Bitbucket repositories.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for unlimited public repositories and one private repository.
caveats: Private repo support limited to one

useCases: Automated code review for open source projects
Integrating code quality checks into CI
quickstartSteps: Sign up at codefactor.io using your GitHub or Bitbucket account.
Authorize CodeFactor to access your repositories.
Select the repository you want to analyze.
Configure analysis settings if needed.
Review the generated code quality report.
entry.whenToUse: Use CodeFactor to automate code quality checks for public repositories or a single private project, especially for open source or small teams.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need analysis for multiple private repositories or require advanced customization, as these require a paid plan.
entry.body: CodeFactor offers automated static code analysis and code review for GitHub and Bitbucket repositories, with a free tier for public and one private repo.

entry.title: CodeKeep
entry.description: CodeKeep is a cloud-based snippet manager for organizing, searching, and sharing code snippets across devices.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan allows storing and organizing a limited number of code snippets with basic search and sync features.
caveats: No team collaboration on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free CodeKeep account at codekeep.io.
Log in and create your first snippet using the web interface.
Organize snippets with tags or folders.
Install browser extension or desktop app if desired.
Sync and access your snippets from multiple devices.
entry.whenToUse: Use CodeKeep when you need to organize, search, and access code snippets from anywhere with cloud sync.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced team collaboration, unlimited storage, or deep IDE integrations on the free tier.
entry.body: CodeKeep helps developers organize and access code snippets in the cloud, offering basic search and sync features on its free tier.

entry.title: CodeRabbit
entry.description: CodeRabbit provides AI-powered code review automation for GitHub pull requests with customizable rules and suggestions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for public repositories and limited private repo usage.
limits: Limited review frequency for private repos
caveats: Private repo usage is restricted on free plan
Some advanced features require paid plan

useCases: Automated code review for open source projects
Improving code quality in small teams
quickstartSteps: Sign up at coderabbit.ai with your GitHub account.
Authorize CodeRabbit to access your repositories.
Select the repositories you want to enable for code review.
Configure review settings and rules as needed.
Create or update a pull request to trigger an automated review.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want automated, AI-driven code reviews for GitHub projects, especially for open source or small private repos.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited private repo support, advanced customization, or if your codebase is not hosted on GitHub.
entry.body: CodeRabbit offers AI-powered automated code reviews for GitHub pull requests, making it easy to improve code quality and reduce manual review effort, especially for open source and small private projects.

entry.title: Export SDK
entry.description: Export SDK provides tools to export data from SaaS apps and databases into various formats for integration and backup.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited data exports per month with access to core SDK features.
limits: Up to 1,000 records exported per month

useCases: Exporting SaaS app data for reporting
Integrating app data into analytics tools
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Export SDK account.
Install the SDK in your project using npm or pip.
Configure your API keys and select a supported SaaS connector.
Write a script to export data using provided SDK methods.
Run your script and verify exported data.
entry.whenToUse: Use Export SDK when you need to automate data exports from SaaS apps or databases for integration or backup purposes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high-volume exports, advanced connectors, or guaranteed support, as these require a paid plan.
entry.body: Export SDK is a developer tool for exporting data from popular SaaS platforms and databases, suitable for light integration and backup tasks.

entry.title: Flagsmith
entry.description: Feature flag and remote config management platform for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier for up to 50,000 requests per month and unlimited feature flags.
caveats: Hosted only (no self-hosted support in free tier)

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Flagsmith account.
Create a new project and environment.
Define feature flags in your environment.
Integrate the Flagsmith SDK into your app.
Use feature flags in your code to control features.
entry.whenToUse: Use Flagsmith when you need to manage feature flags or remote configuration for web or mobile apps with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need on-premise/self-hosted features in the free tier or require advanced enterprise integrations.
entry.body: Flagsmith offers a generous free tier for feature flag management, supporting unlimited flags and environments with a monthly request cap. It's ideal for teams and projects needing remote configuration or experimentation without complex setup. The platform is easy to integrate and supports open-source usage, but advanced enterprise features and self-hosted options require paid plans.

entry.title: GitDailies
entry.description: GitDailies generates automated daily summaries and reports from your GitHub repositories for improved team visibility.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes daily summaries for one repository with basic reporting features.
caveats: Advanced analytics and multi-repo support require paid plan

quickstartSteps: Sign up at gitdailies.com using your GitHub account.
Authorize GitDailies to access your repositories.
Select the repository you want to monitor.
Configure notification preferences and summary schedule.
Receive daily summary emails.
entry.whenToUse: Use GitDailies when you want automated daily summaries of GitHub activity for a single repository without manual setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need multi-repo support, advanced analytics, or integration with non-GitHub platforms.
entry.body: GitDailies provides automated daily summaries from your GitHub repositories, helping teams and individuals stay updated with minimal setup.

entry.title: GitGud
entry.description: GitGud is a free, privacy-focused Git hosting platform supporting repositories, issues, and wikis.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free public and private repositories with no user or storage limits.

quickstartSteps: Go to https://gitgud.io and sign up for a free account.
Verify your email address to activate your account.
Create a new repository from your dashboard.
Clone the repository locally using the provided Git URL.
Push your code and start collaborating.
entry.whenToUse: Use GitGud when you need a free, privacy-respecting Git hosting platform for personal, open-source, or small team projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require enterprise features, official support, or guaranteed uptime for critical production workloads.
entry.body: GitGud offers free, privacy-focused Git hosting with unlimited repositories and users, making it ideal for students, indie developers, and open-source projects.

entry.title: RepoFlow
entry.description: RepoFlow automates GitHub repository workflows, PR management, and code review processes for teams and individuals.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic workflow automation for public and private GitHub repositories.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at repoflow.io using your GitHub account.
Authorize RepoFlow to access your repositories.
Select repositories to connect and configure workflow templates.
Enable desired automations such as PR labeling or review enforcement.
Monitor activity and adjust settings as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use RepoFlow to automate repetitive GitHub repository tasks and enforce consistent PR and code review processes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need automation for non-GitHub platforms or require advanced custom workflows beyond the free tier.
entry.body: RepoFlow helps automate and streamline GitHub repository workflows, making it easier for teams and individuals to manage pull requests and code reviews efficiently.

entry.title: RepoForge
entry.description: RepoForge automates dependency updates and manages pull requests for GitHub repositories with customizable workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier supports up to 3 public or private repositories with core automation features.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at repoforge.io using your GitHub account.
Authorize RepoForge to access your repositories.
Select up to 3 repositories to manage.
Configure automation rules and workflows as needed.
Monitor pull requests and updates from the RepoForge dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use RepoForge to automate dependency updates and pull request workflows for a small number of GitHub repositories.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need to manage more than 3 repositories for free or require integration with platforms other than GitHub.
entry.body: RepoForge provides automated dependency management and pull request workflows for up to three GitHub repositories, making it ideal for individuals and small teams seeking to streamline repository maintenance.

entry.title: ReportGPT
entry.description: Generate AI-powered reports from your data or prompts with customizable templates and export options.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited report generations per month with basic export formats.
caveats: Advanced templates and integrations require paid plan

useCases: Generate quick business or academic reports
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free ReportGPT account at https://ReportGPT.app.
Log in and select 'Create New Report'.
Choose a template or start from scratch.
Input your data or prompt and configure report settings.
Generate and export your report.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to quickly generate structured reports from data or prompts without manual formatting.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require highly customized layouts, advanced integrations, or unlimited usage without upgrading.
entry.body: ReportGPT offers a simple way to generate AI-powered reports from your data or prompts, ideal for students and small teams needing basic reporting automation.

entry.title: Tickgit.com
entry.description: Tickgit scans your codebase for TODO comments and helps you track and manage them efficiently.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories and limited private repositories.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at tickgit.com using your GitHub account.
Authorize Tickgit to access your repositories.
Select repositories to scan for TODOs.
Review and manage TODOs from the Tickgit dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Tickgit when you want to track, search, and manage TODO comments across your codebase, especially for team collaboration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need deep code analysis, custom integrations, or support for non-GitHub repositories.
entry.body: Tickgit helps developers and teams find, track, and manage TODO comments in their codebase, improving visibility and collaboration around technical debt.
